sentence_reviews.text: Autonomous-research systems search literature, formulate hypotheses, design or run experiments, analyze evidence, write artifacts, and request human decisions.

sentence_reviews.text: This system is an application boundary where technical capability, evidence, safety controls, economics, and user workflow must succeed together.

edge_reviews.notes: Autonomous research systems plausibly require agentic software/task execution; support is synthesized from AIMA and structured-output/tooling references.
Experiment design and simulation workflows support this dependency for scientific research systems, though not all autonomous research uses digital twins.
Literature search and evidence analysis are directly aligned with RAG/question-answering sources.
Human decisions and permissions are supported by the node wording plus NIST/formal-methods risk framing.
Longitudinal research agents may use episodic memory, but the packet sources do not establish common strength.
Structured retrieval is plausible for research literature and evidence workflows, but optional.
Structured artifacts/actions are supported by the structured-output survey and agent workflow framing.
Formal verification is a plausible assurance layer, but not clearly supported as a dependency of autonomous research systems generally.
Research artifacts and evidence provenance make this optional dependency defensible; C2PA supports provenance tooling generally.
Extra inference-time reasoning can help, but cited sources do not establish it as a standard autonomous-research dependency.
